data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add `summary` subcommand to the ticket CLI dispatcher that wraps issue-summary.sh's one-line ticket summary with blocking status.

**Why**: The ticket system should provide its own one-line summaries for status reporting without callers invoking a separate script.

**Scope**:
- IN: New `summary` case in dispatcher routing to issue-summary.sh logic; cycle detection for circular block relationships (bounded traversal prevents infinite loops); integration test via dispatcher asserting output matches issue-summary.sh and cycle detection prevents hangs
- OUT: Migrating callers (Epic e1b2-2653); removing issue-summary.sh

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `ticket summary <id>` is callable via the dispatcher and produces the same output as issue-summary.sh including blocking status
  <- Satisfies: "summary (one-line ticket summary with blocking status for one or more ticket IDs -- replaces issue-summary.sh)"
- When this story is complete, cycle detection is implemented such that circular block relationships do not cause infinite loops during summary traversal
  <- Satisfies: "Scenario Analysis: summary on a ticket with circular blocks relationship loops (medium, correctness)"
- When this story is complete, the summary subcommand handles dangling link references gracefully (links pointing to deleted/purged tickets do not crash the subcommand)
  <- Satisfies: cross-story assumption that purge-bridge may leave dangling link targets
- When this story is complete, the integration test exercises summary through the CLI dispatcher against a synthetic fixture and asserts output equivalence with issue-summary.sh
  <- Satisfies: "Each new subcommand has an integration test asserting output equivalence"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Cycle detection required -- circular block relationships must be bounded; reuse existing cycle detection from deps command if available
- [Reliability] Dangling link references (after purge-bridge deletes tickets that are link targets) must not crash summary -- handle gracefully with a warning
- [Maintainability] Depends on S7 (d7d8-736b) landing first so purge-bridge's link-graph mutation behavior is defined before summary relies on graph shape

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: As a developer, I can retrieve a one-line ticket summary with blocking status via ticket summary
